creator: rick@sloservers.com
text:
Have you tried building this under FBSD versions less than 9?  I
encountered numerous build errors that this patch does not appear to
address including: ports flex dependency, newer as dependency from
updated binutils, patch to remove treating certain erroneous warnings as
errors / build failures under FBSD base GCC.

On 2012/08/06 6:58, Norihiko Murase wrote:
>> Number:         170417
>> Category:       ports
>> Synopsis:       Update port: sysutils/grub2 to 2.00
>> Confidential:   no
>> Severity:       non-critical
>> Priority:       low
>> Responsible:    freebsd-ports-bugs
>> State:          open
>> Quarter:        
>> Keywords:       
>> Date-Required:
>> Class:          change-request
>> Submitter-Id:   current-users
>> Arrival-Date:   Mon Aug 06 14:00:09 UTC 2012
>> Closed-Date:
>> Last-Modified:
>> Originator:     Norihiko Murase
>> Release:        FreeBSD 9.0-RELEASE-p3 i386
>> Organization:
>> Environment:
>> Description:
> The first *stable* release of GRUB2 became available on Jun 28 in 2012.
> http://lists.gnu.org/archive/html/grub-devel/2012-06/msg00093.html
>
> I would like you to update the FreeBSD ports for this software 
> ("sysutils/grub2") for this release.
>
> I've created the patch, which covers the following
>   * Update the information of the distfile.
>       - Now the .tar.xz file is used (USE_XZ=yes).
>       - The SHA256 checksum, the file size of it
>   * Update MASTER_SITES: this release is now *stable* one, which is 
>     available at the official GNU site (ftp.gnu.org).
>   * Add LICENSE
>   * Convert to new OPTIONS framework (OptionsNG)
>   * pkg-message: Update the example of menu entry.
>
> !NOTE!: This patch is INCOMPLETE...
>   * pkg-plist: More entries for the .mo files might be required...
>   * pkg-message: Is this example appropriate for this release?
>       - Since v1.99, the name of a partition should have the form 
>         `(hd0,msdos1,bsd1)', rather than `(hd0,1,a)'.
>         From: grub-2.00/NEWS
>   * Is the "NLS" build option necessary?
>   * The patches in files/ is NOT updated for this release.
>
>> How-To-Repeat:
>> Fix:
>
>> Release-Note:
>> Audit-Trail:
>> Unformatted:
> _______________________________________________
> freebsd-ports-bugs@freebsd.org mailing list
> http://lists.freebsd.org/mailman/listinfo/freebsd-ports-bugs
> To unsubscribe, send any mail to "freebsd-ports-bugs-unsubscribe@freebsd.org"
